title: AI-901 Mock Exam 3: End-to-End Scenario Practice
description: Harder unofficial AI-901 scenario set covering Foundry SDK/API use, agent architecture, Content Understanding, governance, quiz-app-adjacent implementation details, and responsible AI. Based on the public Microsoft AI-901 skills guide, Microsoft Foundry, Agent Service, and Content Understanding documentation. Original practice questions; not official exam content.

items.prompt: A developer creates a Foundry prompt agent for facilities requests. It must search building-policy PDFs and submit approved maintenance tickets. Which two tool categories are most relevant?
items.answer: File search and a custom function or API tool
options: File search and a custom function or API tool

items.prompt: A team moves from Foundry classic hub-based projects to the current Foundry project model. What benefit is most aligned with the current platform?
items.answer: Unified management of agents, models, tools, RBAC, networking, and policies
options: Unified management of agents, models, tools, RBAC, networking, and policies

items.prompt: A Python client needs to call a Foundry model through the project OpenAI client. Which sequence is most appropriate?
items.answer: Create AIProjectClient with endpoint and credential, get the OpenAI client, then call responses.create
options: Create AIProjectClient with endpoint and credential, get the OpenAI client, then call responses.create
Create a DNS record, open Notepad, then paste the prompt
Upload a CSS file, then call speech synthesis
Create a blob container and skip authentication

items.prompt: A REST client calls /openai/v1/responses on a Foundry project endpoint. What must it include for secure access?
items.answer: A valid bearer token or supported credential for the Foundry resource/project
options: A valid bearer token or supported credential for the Foundry resource/project
A browser cookie from any website
The user prompt as the only header

items.prompt: A company needs an agent to answer questions from Microsoft 365 and enterprise data with citation-backed answers. Which Foundry capability area should they investigate?
items.answer: Knowledge grounding such as Foundry IQ or connected retrieval tools
options: Knowledge grounding such as Foundry IQ or connected retrieval tools

items.prompt: An agent uses web search, file search, memory, and code interpreter. What service feature provides these built-in capabilities?
items.answer: Foundry Agent Service tools

items.prompt: A developer wants to see metrics and Application Insights data for agent runs. Which Foundry Agent Service area is involved?
items.answer: Observability

items.prompt: A prompt agent is best suited for which scenario?
items.answer: A managed portal-authored assistant with instructions and tools but no custom runtime code
options: A managed portal-authored assistant with instructions and tools but no custom runtime code
A highly customized containerized LangGraph workflow only
A storage encryption key rotation job

items.prompt: A hosted agent is best suited for which scenario?
items.answer: A code-based agent that needs custom orchestration and dependencies
options: A code-based agent that needs custom orchestration and dependencies

items.prompt: A user asks an agent to summarize a spreadsheet and calculate totals. Which tool should the agent use?
items.answer: Code interpreter

items.prompt: A user asks an agent to answer using uploaded HR documents. Which tool should the agent use?
items.answer: File search

items.prompt: A model needs to call many providers through a consistent Foundry contract. Which platform concept supports this?
items.answer: Foundry API and model catalog access
options: Foundry API and model catalog access

items.prompt: A company wants to choose a model for complex multi-step reasoning. Which model capability should be prioritized?
items.answer: Advanced reasoning quality

items.prompt: A company wants an on-device or resource-constrained model. Which model characteristic is most relevant?
items.answer: Small language model suitability

items.prompt: A user asks for "all employee salaries" but the agent should only answer from authorized data. Which controls are most relevant?
items.answer: Identity, RBAC, tool authorization, and retrieval permissions
options: Identity, RBAC, tool authorization, and retrieval permissions

items.prompt: A model uses retrieved paragraphs but does not cite them. The requirement is citation-backed answers. What should change?
items.answer: Configure retrieval/grounding and prompt the model to include source references
options: Configure retrieval/grounding and prompt the model to include source references

items.prompt: A developer is asked to build a lightweight app that includes text analysis. Which flow is most appropriate?
items.answer: Collect text, call a text-capable model or text analysis service, and display extracted insights
options: Collect text, call a text-capable model or text analysis service, and display extracted insights
Capture a microphone recording and stop
Create a DNS record and display it
Generate an image from every sentence

items.prompt: A developer is asked to build a lightweight app with vision capabilities. Which flow is most appropriate?
items.answer: Accept image input, call a vision-capable model or tool, and handle visual results
options: Accept image input, call a vision-capable model or tool, and handle visual results

items.prompt: A developer is asked to build an information extraction app with Content Understanding. Which flow is most appropriate?
items.answer: Create or use an analyzer, submit content, and consume structured extracted fields
options: Create or use an analyzer, submit content, and consume structured extracted fields

items.prompt: A document analyzer returns low confidence for a due date. What should the app do?
items.answer: Flag the field for review or validation instead of blindly accepting it
options: Flag the field for review or validation instead of blindly accepting it
Always overwrite it with today’s date

items.prompt: A model must process both a chart image and a written question about the chart. Which capability is needed?
items.answer: Multimodal input handling

items.prompt: A customer complaint says: "The delivery was late and the box was damaged." Which outputs are most plausible from text analysis?
items.answer: Negative sentiment and key phrases such as delivery late and box damaged
options: Negative sentiment and key phrases such as delivery late and box damaged

items.prompt: A call summarization app is producing inaccurate summaries because the transcript has recognition errors. What should be improved first?
items.answer: Speech recognition quality or transcript correction
options: Speech recognition quality or transcript correction

items.prompt: A multilingual voice bot must respond in the same language used by the speaker. Which capabilities are relevant?
items.answer: Speech recognition with language handling and speech synthesis
options: Speech recognition with language handling and speech synthesis

items.prompt: A model should not answer outside an approved domain. Which prompt instruction is strongest?
items.answer: If the request is outside the approved domain, refuse or redirect and do not guess
options: If the request is outside the approved domain, refuse or redirect and do not guess
Ignore the domain if the user insists

items.prompt: An AI app needs monitoring for latency, failures, safety events, and tool calls. Which lifecycle stage is this?
items.answer: Operate and govern after deployment

items.prompt: A user provides ambiguous input. The assistant lacks enough information to complete a tool action safely. What should it do?
items.answer: Ask a clarifying question before acting
options: Ask a clarifying question before acting

items.prompt: A system prompt and user prompt conflict. Which should have higher priority?
items.answer: System prompt

items.prompt: A developer needs all responses to include "answer" and "confidence" fields. What is the best implementation practice?
items.answer: Specify the schema and validate the response before use
options: Specify the schema and validate the response before use
Ask the model to be creative

items.prompt: A Foundry agent uses a custom function to update orders. Which risk is most important to mitigate?
items.answer: The model may call the function with unsafe or invalid parameters
options: The model may call the function with unsafe or invalid parameters
The UI may have too few colors
The model may not generate images
The response may be too short for a paragraph

items.prompt: A team wants to deploy models at scale with centralized controls. What Foundry capability is most relevant?
items.answer: Unified management with RBAC, policies, monitoring, and model deployments
options: Unified management with RBAC, policies, monitoring, and model deployments

items.prompt: A developer tests a model using only ideal prompts. Which risk remains?
items.answer: The app may fail on edge cases, unsafe prompts, or representative user inputs
options: The app may fail on edge cases, unsafe prompts, or representative user inputs
The app will always be too expensive

items.prompt: An image-generation app lets users create product mockups. Which responsible AI concern should be considered?
items.answer: Misuse, harmful content, copyright/brand policy, and transparency about generated content
options: Misuse, harmful content, copyright/brand policy, and transparency about generated content

items.prompt: A customer support assistant must escalate angry high-risk messages to a human. Which capabilities can help?
items.answer: Sentiment analysis plus workflow/tool integration

items.prompt: A model deployment returns different phrasing each time, causing snapshot tests to fail. Which setting helps reduce variation?
items.answer: Lower temperature

items.prompt: A model must process very long policy documents. Which design issue should the developer consider?
items.answer: Context limits and retrieval/chunking strategy

items.prompt: A financial assistant must explain why a recommendation was made and what sources were used. Which practices help?
items.answer: Source grounding, citations, transparent limitations, and logs for review
options: Source grounding, citations, transparent limitations, and logs for review

items.prompt: A developer wants the model to choose between calling a search tool and a calculator tool. What must be provided?
items.answer: Clear tool definitions, instructions, and access to both tools
options: Clear tool definitions, instructions, and access to both tools

items.prompt: A team needs to prevent users from uploading malicious content that causes the model to ignore instructions. Which risk is this?
items.answer: Prompt injection through untrusted content

items.prompt: How should an app handle instructions found inside retrieved documents?
items.answer: Treat retrieved content as untrusted data and keep system instructions authoritative
options: Treat retrieved content as untrusted data and keep system instructions authoritative
Let document text override system instructions

items.prompt: A model is used to classify support tickets into billing, technical, or account categories. Which metric should be reviewed across real examples?
items.answer: Classification accuracy or error analysis by category
options: Classification accuracy or error analysis by category

items.prompt: A team wants to keep public submissions hidden until review. Which combination is correct?
items.answer: Store submissions as pending and filter public gallery to approved items
options: Store submissions as pending and filter public gallery to approved items
Display pending items publicly with no review
Require every user to be an admin

items.prompt: A dataset upload includes multiple-choice items where the correct answer is always first. What should the quiz UI do?
items.answer: Shuffle answer options at runtime while preserving the correct answer mapping
options: Shuffle answer options at runtime while preserving the correct answer mapping

items.prompt: A user accidentally clicks the wrong option in a multiple-choice quiz. Which interaction reduces this risk?
items.answer: Select an option first, then require explicit confirmation
options: Select an option first, then require explicit confirmation

items.prompt: A flashcard asks a user to recall an answer. What is the fair scoring flow?
items.answer: Reveal the answer, then let the user self-report whether they got it
options: Reveal the answer, then let the user self-report whether they got it
Award a point immediately on reveal

items.prompt: A team wants randomized question order only for datasets that request it. Where should this preference be stored?
items.answer: As a dataset-level configuration such as shuffleQuestions
options: As a dataset-level configuration such as shuffleQuestions

items.prompt: A lightweight client app must handle a pending upload response. What should it tell the uploader?
items.answer: The dataset was submitted and will appear after admin approval
options: The dataset was submitted and will appear after admin approval
The upload key is the admin password

items.prompt: An AI app should not expose service account keys in frontend code. Where should privileged Firebase or Azure credentials live?
items.answer: Server-side environment variables or managed identity
options: Server-side environment variables or managed identity

items.prompt: A developer wants to test hands-on Foundry skills for AI-901. Which activity is most aligned?
items.answer: Deploy a model, call it from a lightweight app, create an agent, and use Foundry Tools
options: Deploy a model, call it from a lightweight app, create an agent, and use Foundry Tools

items.prompt: A solution must extract values from a video and then let an agent answer questions about those extracted values. Which design is most appropriate?
items.answer: Use Content Understanding for extraction and ground the agent on the structured output
options: Use Content Understanding for extraction and ground the agent on the structured output
Skip extraction and ask the model to guess
